title: set command handler uses imperative tag scan in eval_stmt
description: The set command arm in eval_stmt (evaluator.rs:142-161) uses a manual while-loop with index i to collect leading Tag forms into modifier_names, then pattern-matches rest.get(i) and rest.get(i+1) to get name/value. This is fine but slightly verbose. Idiomatic rewrite: use iterators. `let (modifier_forms, str_forms): (Vec<_>, Vec<_>) = rest.iter().partition(|f| matches!(f, Form::Tag(_)));` then extract name/value from str_forms[0] and str_forms[1]. Or: `let mut it = rest.iter().skip_while(|f| matches!(f, Form::Tag(_)));` then `it.next()` twice. The current approach is not wrong, but the explicit while/index pattern is more C-like than Rustlike here.
